steps.title: Initial Setup/Scanning
steps.instruction: Create comprehensive inventory of all access points and credentials across network
steps.tip: Start with the most reliable information source for vulnerability chaining framework
steps.expected_output: Spreadsheet mapping each host with associated access methods and credentials

steps.title: Data Collection/Configuration
steps.instruction: Design graph visualization with hosts as nodes and access/trust relationships as edges
steps.tip: Organize data systematically to avoid duplicates and ensure coverage
steps.expected_output: Attack graph showing how compromised systems connect and enable progression

steps.title: Analysis/Enumeration
steps.instruction: Test credential reuse across discovered hosts to enable lateral movement
steps.tip: Look for patterns and correlations in collected data
steps.expected_output: Confirmed list of credentials working across multiple systems

steps.title: Advanced Technique/Filtering
steps.instruction: Document complete attack chain from initial access to final objective
steps.tip: Use multiple techniques to increase coverage and accuracy
steps.expected_output: Step-by-step narrative documenting progression through multiple systems

steps.title: Correlation/Testing
steps.instruction: Identify detection opportunities and security controls bypassed at each stage
steps.tip: Validate findings across different tools and methods
steps.expected_output: Analysis of detection gaps and security control weaknesses exploited

steps.title: Validation/Exploitation
steps.instruction: Create visual storyboard presenting attack chain as narrative progression
steps.expected_output: Client-ready presentation of the complete attack story with visual flow
